verses.text: To Sardis: 'thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.' Be watchful, strengthen what remains, 'for I have not found thy works perfect.' Dead name. A reputation for life is not life itself.
To Sardis: 'thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.' Be watchful, strengthen what remains, 'for I have not found thy works perfect.' Dead name. A reputation for life is not life itself.
To Sardis: 'thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.' Be watchful, strengthen what remains, 'for I have not found thy works perfect.' Dead name. A reputation for life is not life itself.
A few in Sardis have 'not defiled their garments'; the overcomer shall be 'clothed in white,' his name kept in 'the book of life.' White robes. The faithful few will walk with Christ.
A few in Sardis have 'not defiled their garments'; the overcomer shall be 'clothed in white,' his name kept in 'the book of life.' White robes. The faithful few will walk with Christ.
A few in Sardis have 'not defiled their garments'; the overcomer shall be 'clothed in white,' his name kept in 'the book of life.' White robes. The faithful few will walk with Christ.
To Philadelphia, the holy and true, who 'openeth, and no man shutteth': 'I have set before thee an open door.' Open door. Christ opens what none can shut for the faithful.
To Philadelphia, the holy and true, who 'openeth, and no man shutteth': 'I have set before thee an open door.' Open door. Christ opens what none can shut for the faithful.
Because thou hast 'kept the word of my patience,' I will keep thee 'from the hour of temptation' coming on the world. Kept. The patient are preserved in trial.
Because thou hast 'kept the word of my patience,' I will keep thee 'from the hour of temptation' coming on the world. Kept. The patient are preserved in trial.
'Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast.' The overcomer is made 'a pillar in the temple of my God,' bearing God's name. Made a pillar. The faithful are fixed forever in God's house.
'Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast.' The overcomer is made 'a pillar in the temple of my God,' bearing God's name. Made a pillar. The faithful are fixed forever in God's house.
'Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast.' The overcomer is made 'a pillar in the temple of my God,' bearing God's name. Made a pillar. The faithful are fixed forever in God's house.
To Laodicea, 'the faithful and true witness': 'thou art neither cold nor hot... I will spue thee out.' Lukewarm. Half-hearted faith is loathsome to Christ.
To Laodicea, 'the faithful and true witness': 'thou art neither cold nor hot... I will spue thee out.' Lukewarm. Half-hearted faith is loathsome to Christ.
To Laodicea, 'the faithful and true witness': 'thou art neither cold nor hot... I will spue thee out.' Lukewarm. Half-hearted faith is loathsome to Christ.
'Thou sayest, I am rich... and knowest not that thou art... poor, and blind, and naked.' Buy of Me gold and white raiment. False wealth. Self-sufficiency hides spiritual poverty.
'Thou sayest, I am rich... and knowest not that thou art... poor, and blind, and naked.' Buy of Me gold and white raiment. False wealth. Self-sufficiency hides spiritual poverty.
'As many as I love, I rebuke': 'Behold, I stand at the door, and knock'; if any open, 'I will come in.' Knocking Christ. He waits to enter and sup with the willing.
'As many as I love, I rebuke': 'Behold, I stand at the door, and knock'; if any open, 'I will come in.' Knocking Christ. He waits to enter and sup with the willing.
'To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne,' as I overcame. Enthroned. The victor shares the throne of Christ.
